+## 18. Delegated-human auto-publish policy
+
+This section makes the bounded conditions under which `-PublishMode main` may
+run legible and auditable. It is **policy documentation only**. Nothing in this
+section enables, registers, schedules, runs, or defaults `-PublishMode main`,
+and nothing here weakens an existing stop condition, finite cap, verification
+gate, control gate, or human-review boundary documented elsewhere in this
+file. It complements — does not replace — §7.1 (unattended operation), §14
+(known gotchas), and §17.4 (current operating policy).
+
+### 18.1 Default mode and the meaning of delegated-human authorization
+
+- **`-PublishMode branch` is the default and the safest mode** for
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`. A passing dispatch leaves the work on its
+  per-issue `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-<n>` branch for a human to review, push, open a
+  PR, and merge. The autonomous loop never touches `origin/main` in this mode.
+- **`-PublishMode main` is not a standing authorization, not a recommended
+  default, and not a scheduler behavior.** It is allowed only as an
+  **explicit, bounded, opt-in human authorization** for a single named batch
+  of work. The human reviewer inspects the batch scope and gates first,
+  selects `-PublishMode main` for that batch only, and lets the batch run to
+  its finite cap; the next batch requires a fresh authorization.
+- **A "delegated-human" run** is therefore: one human, one named batch, one
+  finite cap, one review of the scope and gates beforehand, one auditable
+  trail afterwards. It is not "set and forget," it is not blanket approval
+  for all future tasks, and it is not a recurring schedule.
+- **Repeated bounded batches are the only meaning of "indefinite" operation.**
+  Running the autonomous loop "for a while" means a human authorizes batch 1,
+  reviews the result, authorizes batch 2, and so on. It never means removing
+  the finite cap or a stop condition.
+
+### 18.2 Allowed publish surfaces
+
+Not every surface is appropriate for auto-publish even under delegated-human
+authorization. Surfaces split into two risk tiers:
+
+- **Lower-risk surfaces** (most appropriate for `-PublishMode main` under a
+  delegated-human authorization):
+  - Documentation-only changes (`*.md` outside `ai_handoffs/` /
+    `ai_dispatch_logs/` audit artifacts, ADRs, retrospectives) whose diff is
+    fully contained and easily reverted.
+  - Test-only additions or regression-coverage changes whose diff is confined
+    to test crates / test modules and does not touch production code.
+
+- **Higher-risk surfaces** (default to `-PublishMode branch` and a
+  human-owned PR + merge path, even when a delegated-human authorization is
+  in effect for the batch):
+  - Production Rust source under `crates/**`, `kernel/**`, `editor/**`,
+    `tools/**`.
+  - Scripts (`*.ps1` at the repo root, including
+    `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1` / `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` /
+    `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` / `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1` /
+    `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1` / `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1` /
+    `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1` / `new-handoff.ps1`).
+  - GitHub Actions workflows under `.github/workflows/**`.
+  - JSON schemas under `.ai/*.schema.json`.
+  - Scheduler configuration (registered Windows Scheduled Tasks, the
+    `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1` artifacts).
+  - Dependency edges and Cargo metadata: `Cargo.toml`, `Cargo.lock`, every
+    `**/Cargo.toml`.
+  - The autonomous task brief `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md` and the canonical
+    verification gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`.
+
+A delegated-human authorization SHOULD scope `-PublishMode main` to the
+lower-risk surface. A batch that drifts into a higher-risk surface mid-run
+SHOULD trip a stop condition (see §18.4) rather than silently auto-publish.
+
+### 18.3 Cap rules
+
+Every cap that bounds a single dispatch or a single autonomous batch remains
+finite under delegated-human authorization. None of them may be raised to
+"unlimited" or removed in the name of running longer unattended.
+
+- **`-MaxAutonomousTasks`** (on `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`) MUST remain a
+  finite integer. The auto driver halts the batch after that many issues are
+  filed and processed; the next batch requires a fresh human authorization.
+- **`-MaxPlanRevisions`** (on `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, 0–5, default `1`)
+  MUST remain finite. Once exhausted, the dispatch aborts rather than loop.
+- **`-MaxCorrectionRounds`** (on `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, 0–5, default
+  `1`; queue/auto layers default `2`) MUST remain finite. Once exhausted, the
+  dispatch aborts rather than loop.
+- **One issue, one branch, one dispatch per queue tick.** The single-run lock
+  in `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1` is not waived.
+- **"Indefinite" autonomous operation** is **only** the pattern: human
+  authorizes a finite batch → batch runs to its cap → human reviews →
+  human authorizes the next finite batch. The caps themselves stay finite
+  forever.
+
+### 18.4 Stop conditions
+
+The autonomous loop MUST halt the current batch — without auto-publishing
+the in-flight dispatch and without silently advancing to the next task — when
+any of the following occurs. These are minimums; a delegated-human
+authorization may add more, never fewer.
+
+- The canonical verification gate `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1` exits non-zero
+  (CI would not pass).
+- Codex control returns `needs_changes` beyond `-MaxCorrectionRounds`, or
+  returns `block` at any point.
+- The Claude plan gate returns `needs_changes` beyond `-MaxPlanRevisions`, or
+  returns `block`.
+- Scope drift: the diff touches paths the TASK packet listed as MUST NOT
+  edit, or expands beyond MAY edit.
+- Dirty or unexpected diffs: tracked files outside the dispatch's declared
+  surface are modified; unexpected untracked files appear; a `.meta.json`
+  sidecar is missing where one is required, or present where the TASK forbids
+  it.
+- Missing audit artifacts: the queue audit log
+  (`ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md`), the EXEC packet, or the control verdict
+  cannot be located for the run.
+- Branch / base mismatch: the dispatch is not on the expected
+  `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-<n>` branch, or `origin/main` has diverged from the
+  expected base, or a fast-forward into `main` is no longer possible.
+- Auth or remote failure: `git push`, `gh`, `claude`, or `codex` fails on
+  auth, network, or rate limits.
+- Any attempted change to scheduler configuration, default
+  `-PublishMode`, the canonical verification gate, schemas, workflows, or
+  any other higher-risk surface from §18.2 that the batch was not explicitly
+  authorized to touch.
+- Any attempted edit to a file the TASK packet enumerated as forbidden, or
+  to an existing handoff/log artifact the TASK packet did not authorize.
+
+A halt under any of these conditions MUST leave the work on its branch for
+human review, MUST NOT fast-forward `main`, and MUST NOT proceed to the next
+batch task without a fresh human authorization.
+
+### 18.5 Rollback behavior
+
+Delegated-human authorization does not waive normal rollback discipline.
+
+- **Unpushed local changes** (work that halted before the auto-publish step,
+  or work on an `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-<n>` branch that was committed locally but
+  not fast-forwarded into `main`):
+  - Leave the branch in place for human inspection by default. The work,
+    packets, and audit log are the diagnostic record.
+  - A human may reset, drop, rename, or salvage the branch as appropriate
+    (see §14.8 for salvage label hygiene and §14.12 for positive-salvage of
+    work blocked by an over-broad task gate).
+  - The autonomous loop MUST NOT silently overwrite, force-reset, or discard
+    a halted branch.
+- **Already-published `main` updates** (a passing dispatch that fast-forwarded
+  into `origin/main` before a downstream problem was discovered):
+  - Stop further autonomous batches immediately — revoke the delegated-human
+    authorization until the regression is understood.
+  - Use **normal git / GitHub revert and audit practice**:
+    `git revert <commit>` or a PR-based revert on top of `main`, with a
+    descriptive commit message and an issue/PR link explaining the rollback.
+    Do not rewrite history on `main`, do not force-push `main`, do not
+    silently mutate published commits.
+  - Cross-reference the rollback in the original dispatch's issue, the
+    autonomous task brief if relevant, and any retrospective.
+- In both cases, the rollback path is the same path a human would take by
+  hand; the automation has no special privilege to bypass it.
+
+### 18.6 Audit requirements
+
+A delegated-human authorization MUST produce a complete, durable audit trail
+for the batch — separate from any chat or terminal scrollback. Each
+autonomous tick that processed an issue MUST leave:
+
+- A committed `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` queue audit log on the
+  dispatch's branch, including the file change list, generated artifacts,
+  Claude marker summary, Codex control JSON, and loop output.
+- The dispatch's handoff packets in `ai_handoffs/`: at minimum the TASK
+  packet and at least one EXEC packet, plus any CORRECTION packets the
+  orchestrator wrote for the run (and the matching `.meta.json` sidecars
+  where the packet permits them).
+- The Codex control verdict JSON (`verdict`, `commit_readiness`,
+  `verification`, `required_fixes`, `changed_files`, `commands_run`) for
+  the run, either via the queue log's inlined block or in the run's scratch
+  directory under `.ai/dispatch-<DispatchId>/`.
+- The verification gate output for the run (the `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`
+  result that the orchestrator captured).
+- The full linkage chain: the GitHub issue number, the
+  `ai-dispatch/ISSUE-<n>` branch, the commit SHA(s) the dispatch landed,
+  and — if `-PublishMode main` published it — the fast-forward into
+  `origin/main`.
+
+In addition, the **human authorization itself MUST be recorded** for each
+bounded batch. At minimum:
+
+- Who authorized the batch.
+- The batch's named scope (which task IDs / issues, which surfaces).
+- The cap (`-MaxAutonomousTasks` value used for that batch).
+- The publish mode selected (`branch` or `main`).
+- A note that the scope and gates were reviewed beforehand.
+
+This authorization record can live in the issue comments, the dispatch
+brief, a commit message, a retrospective, or another committed artifact —
+but it MUST be retrievable from `git` / `gh` history after the fact.
+Verbal-only or chat-only authorizations are not sufficient for a
+delegated-human run.
+
+### 18.7 What this dispatch does and does not do
+
+This dispatch (`ISSUE-208`) is a documentation-only change to
+`AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`. Explicitly:
+
+- It does **not** enable `-PublishMode main` anywhere.
+- It does **not** register, modify, run, or test a Windows Scheduled Task.
+- It does **not** edit `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`,
+  `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`,
+  `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`, `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`,
+  `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`, `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`,
+  `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`, `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`, any
+  `.ai/*.schema.json`, any GitHub Actions workflow, any Cargo metadata,
+  any Rust crate or test, or any existing handoff/log artifact.
+- It does **not** change a default `-PublishMode` anywhere, and does
+  **not** imply a standing authorization for `-PublishMode main`.
+- It does **not** broaden the autonomous queue's behavior, the
+  scheduler's behavior, the orchestrator's behavior, or the
+  verification gate's behavior.
+
+The policy documented above takes effect only when a human, in a future
+session, both reviews the batch scope and gates and explicitly invokes
+`Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` with the corresponding `-PublishMode` /
+`-MaxAutonomousTasks` arguments for that batch.
+
+---
+
 *This document describes `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`,
 and the surrounding packet protocol. The inner loop automates model routing; the
 outer queue runner commits and auto-publishes only successful control-passed

+# Execution Report
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-208
+AUTHOR: Executor / Claude
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_14-15-02+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md — added §18 "Delegated-human auto-publish policy"; added §18 entry to TOC; added §18 cross-reference in §7.1
+STATUS: AWAITING_REVIEW
+
+## Task Packet Reference
+
+`ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_TASK_2026-05-26_14-07-26+0300.md`
+
+## Pre-Execution Review Consumed
+
+No pre-execution review issued for this dispatch.
+
+## What I Changed
+
+### Source
+
+- None. This dispatch did not modify any source, script, schema, workflow,
+  Cargo metadata, Rust crate, Rust test, scheduler artifact, task brief,
+  sandbox worktree, or existing handoff/log artifact.
+
+### Tests
+
+- None. This is a documentation-only dispatch.
+
+### Docs
+
+- `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`:
+  - Table of contents: added entry `18. Delegated-human auto-publish policy`.
+  - §7.1 (Unattended operation): added a cross-reference sentence in the
+    `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1` paragraph pointing at §18 and reaffirming
+    `-PublishMode branch` as the default and safest mode. Did not change
+    the surrounding description of the three unattended-operation layers.
+  - §18 (new section): documents the delegated-human auto-publish policy
+    in seven subsections:
+    - §18.1 — default mode and the meaning of delegated-human
+      authorization. States `-PublishMode branch` as default/safest;
+      `-PublishMode main` as explicit, bounded, opt-in human authorization
+      for a single named batch; defines "indefinite" operation as repeated
+      bounded batches, never removal of caps.
+    - §18.2 — allowed publish surfaces. Distinguishes lower-risk
+      (docs / test-only) surfaces from higher-risk (production Rust source,
+      scripts, workflows, schemas, scheduler configuration, dependency and
+      Cargo metadata, the canonical verification gate, the autonomous task
+      brief) surfaces.
+    - §18.3 — cap rules. Reaffirms finite `-MaxAutonomousTasks`, finite
+      `-MaxPlanRevisions`, finite `-MaxCorrectionRounds`, the queue
+      single-run lock, and the bounded-batches definition of indefinite
+      operation.
+    - §18.4 — stop conditions. Enumerates failed canonical verification,
+      failed/blocked Codex control, failed/blocked Claude plan gate, scope
+      drift, dirty or unexpected diffs, missing audit artifacts, branch /
+      base mismatch, auth or remote failure, scheduler / default-mode
+      changes, and any attempted edit to forbidden surfaces. States that a
+      halt under any of these must not fast-forward `main` and must not
+      auto-advance to the next batch task without fresh authorization.
+    - §18.5 — rollback behavior. Covers unpushed local changes (leave on
+      branch for human review; salvage allowed per §14.8 / §14.12; no
+      silent overwrite) and already-published `main` updates (stop further
+      batches; use normal `git revert` / PR revert / audit practice; no
+      history rewrite; no force-push).
+    - §18.6 — audit requirements. Requires the committed queue audit log,
+      handoff packets, control verdict JSON, verification gate output,
+      and full issue/branch/commit linkage per run, plus a retrievable
+      authorization record (who, scope, cap, mode, review note) per batch.
+    - §18.7 — what this dispatch does and does not do. Explicitly states
+      that ISSUE-208 does not enable `-PublishMode main`, does not register
+      / modify / run a schedule, does not edit any listed forbidden
+      surface, does not change any default `-PublishMode`, and does not
+      imply a standing authorization.
+  - Closing italic footer left intact.
+
+## Per-File Summary
+
+Only one file was edited; the bullet list above already enumerates the
+changes per subsection.
+
+## Verification Results
+
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` (pre-edit) → showed only the
+  inbound TASK packet + sidecar as untracked; no pre-existing tracked
+  modifications. Documented before any edit.
+- `git diff --check` → exit 0 (no whitespace errors).
+- `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` → exit 0; printed
+  `VERIFY OK: all 7 verification step(s) passed.` Steps run, in order:
+  `cargo +nightly fmt --check`, `architecture lints`,
+  `architecture-lint test suite`, `cargo deny check`,
+  `cargo test --workspace --all-targets --no-fail-fast -j 1`,
+  `cargo test --workspace --doc`,
+  `cargo bench -p rge-script-bench --no-run`.
+- `git diff --name-only -- . ':(exclude)ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_*' ':(exclude)ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md'`
+  → output: `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` (exactly the expected single line).
+- `git status --short --untracked-files=all` (post-edit, before writing this

+# Task Packet
+
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-208
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+TIMESTAMP: 2026-05-26_14-07-26+0300
+RELATED_FILES:
+- AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.md
+- ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.meta.json
+- ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md
+STATUS: OPEN
+
+## Goal
+
+Document the delegated-human auto-publish policy requested by GitHub issue #208 in `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`. The update must make `PublishMode main` a bounded, explicit, human-delegated authorization mode rather than a default or scheduler behavior, while preserving `PublishMode branch` as the default and safest operating mode.
+
+## Scope
+
+### MAY edit
+- `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`
+- This dispatch's generated execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.md`, if the executor writes or finalizes it as part of the handoff protocol.
+- This dispatch's generated execution-report sidecar matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.meta.json`, if `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize` creates it.
+- The queue-generated audit log matching `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` for `ISSUE-208` only, if the queue runner creates it.
+
+### MUST NOT edit
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`
+- `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`
+- `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`
+- `Get-AiDispatchHealth.ps1`
+- `Watch-AiDispatch.ps1`
+- `Wait-GitHubActions.ps1`
+- `.ai/dispatch.verify.ps1`
+- `.ai/dispatch.tasks.md`
+- `.ai/*.schema.json`
+- `.github/workflows/**`
+- `Cargo.toml`
+- `Cargo.lock`
+- `**/Cargo.toml`
+- `crates/**`
+- `kernel/**`
+- `editor/**`
+- `tools/**`
+- `tests/**`
+- `OLD/**`
+- `.ai/dispatch-*/worktree/**`
+- Any source file, Rust test, Cargo metadata file, workflow, schema, script, scheduler configuration, task brief, existing dispatch artifact, sandbox worktree, status file, ADR, or documentation file other than `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`.
+- Any existing handoff packet, including this TASK packet.
+- Any existing `ai_dispatch_logs/**` artifact.
+
+### MAY add new files
+- Exactly one execution report matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.md`.
+- The matching execution-report sidecar matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.meta.json`, only if produced by `new-handoff.ps1 -Finalize`.
+- At most one queue-generated audit log matching `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` whose content identifies `ISSUE-208`.
+
+### MUST NOT add new files
+- New scripts, schemas, workflows, source files, Rust tests, Cargo files, task-brief files, scheduler artifacts, sandbox worktrees, ADRs, status docs, generated fixtures, screenshots, review packets, correction packets, closeout packets, or any handoff/log artifact not belonging to `ISSUE-208`.
+
+## Deliverables
+
+- A focused documentation change in `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` that defines delegated-human auto-publish policy for autonomous dispatch operation.
+- The documentation must state that `-PublishMode branch` remains the default and safest mode.
+- The documentation must state that `-PublishMode main` is allowed only as explicit, bounded, opt-in human authorization for a finite batch of work.
+- The documentation must describe the delegated-human risk model, including allowed surfaces, cap rules, stop conditions, rollback behavior, and audit requirements.
+- The documentation must state that `-MaxAutonomousTasks` remains finite. Any "indefinite" operation must mean repeated bounded batches authorized by a human, not removing finite caps or stop conditions.
+- The documentation must make clear that this dispatch does not enable, register, schedule, run, or default `-PublishMode main`.
+- One `EXECUTION_REPORT` packet under `ai_handoffs/` documenting the changed sections, verification results, and static scope inspection.
+
+## Acceptance Criteria
+
+- `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` contains an explicit policy for delegated-human auto-publish.
+- The policy treats delegated-human mode as bounded opt-in authorization for `-PublishMode main`, not as unattended blanket approval.
+- The policy says `-PublishMode branch` is the default and safest mode.
+- The policy documents allowed publish surfaces. At minimum, it must distinguish lower-risk docs/test-only work from production-source, script, workflow, schema, scheduler, dependency, and Cargo surfaces.
+- The policy documents cap rules, including finite `-MaxAutonomousTasks`, finite correction/plan rounds, and repeated human-authorized batches as the only meaning of indefinite operation.
+- The policy documents stop conditions, including failed verification, failed or blocked Codex control, scope drift, dirty or unexpected diffs, missing audit artifacts, branch/base mismatch, auth or remote failure, scheduler/default-mode changes, and any attempted edit to forbidden surfaces.
+- The policy documents rollback behavior for both unpushed local changes and already pushed `main` updates, including stopping further autonomous batches and using normal git/GitHub audit and revert practices rather than silent history rewrites.
+- The policy documents audit requirements, including committed queue logs, handoff packets, control verdicts, verification output, issue/branch/commit linkage, and human review of each bounded batch authorization.
+- No script, schema, workflow, Rust source, test, Cargo file, task brief, existing handoff/log artifact, scheduler registration, or sandbox worktree is changed.
+- No default is changed to `-PublishMode main`, and no schedule is enabled, registered, modified, or run by this dispatch.
+- The executor's final status inspection proves that the only changed tracked file outside this dispatch's generated handoff/log artifacts is `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`.
+
+## Constraints / Non-Goals
+
+- This is a documentation-only dispatch plus its own generated handoff/log artifacts.
+- Do not edit automation behavior.
+- Do not edit `Invoke-AiDispatchAuto.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchQueue.ps1`, `Invoke-AiDispatchLoop.ps1`, `Register-AiDispatchSchedule.ps1`, or any other script.
+- Do not edit schemas, workflows, Rust source, tests, Cargo files, task briefs, scheduler configuration, sandbox worktrees, or existing artifacts.
+- Do not register, enable, modify, run, or test a Windows Scheduled Task.
+- Do not run the autonomous queue or issue selector.
+- Do not push, merge, open a PR, create a GitHub issue comment, or change labels.
+- Do not set, recommend setting as a default, or imply a standing authorization for `-PublishMode main`.
+- Do not remove or weaken existing stop conditions, finite caps, verification gates, control gates, or human review boundaries.
+- Do not broaden this into a scheduler implementation, schema change, queue behavior change, or task-brief rewrite.
+
+## Verification Gates
+
+The Executor MUST run and document the result of each gate in the `EXECUTION_REPORT`:
+
+- Before editing, run `git status --short --untracked-files=all` and document any pre-existing changes. Do not clean, revert, or modify unrelated changes.
+- Inspect the relevant current documentation sections in `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`, including the table of contents, unattended operation, parameters, operational notes, and current operating policy. Preserve existing structure where practical.
+- After editing, run `git diff --check`, expected exit 0.
+- Run the canonical verification gate `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1`, expected exit 0.
+- Run `git diff --name-only -- . ':(exclude)ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_*' ':(exclude)ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md'` and document the output. Expected output: exactly `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`.
+- Run `git status --short --untracked-files=all` after writing the EXEC report and document every line. Expected: no executor-created changes outside `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md`, this dispatch's `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.md` report, the optional matching `ai_handoffs/ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.meta.json` sidecar, and any queue-generated `ai_dispatch_logs/log_*.md` artifact for `ISSUE-208`.
+- Statically inspect the final diff for forbidden-surface edits and state in the EXEC report that no scripts, schemas, workflows, Rust source, Rust tests, Cargo files, task briefs, scheduler configuration, existing artifacts, or sandbox worktrees were changed.
+- Confirm the final documentation includes each required concept: delegated-human opt-in for `-PublishMode main`, branch mode as default/safest, allowed surfaces, cap rules, stop conditions, rollback behavior, audit requirements, and finite `-MaxAutonomousTasks`.
+
+## Halt Conditions
+
+The Executor MUST halt without committing and request fresh guidance if any of the following occur:
+
+- The documentation update appears to require changing any script, schema, workflow, Rust source, test, Cargo file, task brief, scheduler configuration, existing artifact, or sandbox worktree.
+- Any command, tool, or reviewer asks the Executor to enable, register, run, or modify a schedule.
+- Any command, tool, or reviewer asks the Executor to run the autonomous queue or default `-PublishMode main`.
+- The current code or documentation contradicts the requested policy in a way that cannot be documented truthfully without implementation changes.
+- The canonical verification gate `.\.ai\dispatch.verify.ps1` fails for a reason outside the allowed documentation edit surface.
+- `git diff --check` reports whitespace errors outside the allowed documentation edit.
+- Static inspection shows any executor-created change outside `AI_DISPATCH_AUTOMATION.md` and this dispatch's generated handoff/log artifacts.
+- A pre-existing dirty worktree makes it impossible to prove the executor-created scope boundary.
+- More than one documentation file would need to change to satisfy the goal.
+
+If a halt condition fires after any work has begun, the Executor must write the `EXECUTION_REPORT` if possible, set header `STATUS: BLOCKED` or `STATUS: NEEDS_HUMAN` as appropriate, include the exact halt reason and current status output, and route the footer to `NEXT_ROLE: PLANNER_AI` or `NEXT_ROLE: HUMAN_ARBITER`.
+
+## Planner Notes
+
+The requested change is policy documentation only. The intent is to make a high-risk mode legible and auditable, not to make it easier to run by accident. Keep the language operational and bounded: a human may delegate `-PublishMode main` for a finite, named batch after reviewing the scope and gates; the automation must still stop on failures, caps, control blocks, scope drift, or missing audit evidence.
+
+Under the orchestrator protocol, no `_REVIEW_` or `_CLOSEOUT_` packet is expected from this TASK. The only new packet produced by execution should be the `ISSUE-208_EXEC_*.md` report, plus any sidecar/log artifact generated by the tooling for this dispatch.
+
+---
+
+HANDOFF_STATUS: COMPLETE
+DISPATCH_ID: ISSUE-208
+AUTHOR: Planner / OpenAI Codex
+NEXT_ROLE: EXECUTOR_AI
+EXIT_CODE: 0
+
+---
